title: carya alba subsp. tomentosa
intro: Carya alba subsp. tomentosa, also known as the Eastern White Walnut or Hairy-Shell White Walnut, is a subspecies of the Eastern White Walnut tree, characterized by its hairy shell and rich in nutrients. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of juglone and other polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Carya alba subsp. tomentosa include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or oil, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems.
